channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: Implementation of the Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) bundled payment model in the US led to a significant decrease in the proportion of hospitals delivering prehabilitation for lower extremity joint replacement, with a difference-in-differences analysis showing a clear negative effect.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Beyond "Biomedical-Plus": Reclaiming the biopsychosocial model in musculoskeletal physiotherapy.
item.description: This perspective piece argues that the biopsychosocial (BPS) model in musculoskeletal physiotherapy often defaults to a "biomedical-plus" approach, suggesting that deeper integration requires professional confidence, role clarity, and compassionate communication (the 3Cs).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effectiveness of Combined Biopsychosocial Interventions for Chronic Low Back Pain: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis evaluated combined biopsychosocial interventions for chronic low back pain, showing effectiveness in reducing pain and functional disability compared to usual care, waiting lists, or unimodal interventions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Comparative efficacy of different joint mobilization techniques in shoulder adhesive capsulitis: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis compared the clinical effects of different non-thrust joint mobilization approaches for shoulder adhesive capsulitis, analyzing randomized controlled trials published between 2000 and 2025.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effectiveness of an individualised swimming and education programme for chronic low back pain (EduSwim): a randomised controlled trial.
item.description: This RCT assessed an individualized swimming and education program for chronic low back pain compared to education alone, delivered via telehealth sessions over 8 weeks, with disability (RMDQ) as the primary outcome.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: APTA Members Unite to Advance Payment Advocacy Via New SPARC Forum and Pledge
item.description: APTA members are uniting to advance payment advocacy through the newly formed SPARC Forum and a pledge to support these efforts.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Effectiveness of different modes of digital health interventions for sarcopenia in older adults: a systematic review and network meta-analysis | BMC Geriatrics
item.description: A systematic review and network meta-analysis evaluated the effectiveness of different modes of digital health interventions for sarcopenia in older adults.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Outpatient rehabilitation can prevent suicide after acquired brain injury.
item.description: A registry study of 591 patients with acquired brain injury found that outpatient rehabilitation can prevent suicide, showing a higher frequency of suicide after stroke and traumatic brain injury than in the general population.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: [Correspondence] From targets to minutes: making movement advice actionable
item.description: This correspondence emphasizes shifting physical activity messaging from aspirational thresholds to achievable marginal gains, suggesting that small increases in activity among the least active yield the greatest population benefit, aligning with WHO guidance.
item.author: The Lancet

item.title: From mechanics to mobilization: a biomechanically-informed rehabilitation framework for cesarean-related scars
item.description: A systematic review proposed a biomechanically-informed rehabilitation framework for cesarean-related scars, integrating strategies for both abdominal wall and uterine scars, addressing a lack of standardization in current approaches.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: A systematic review of current evidence on clinical outcomes of lumbar roll and modified SIMS manipulation techniques in sacroiliac joint dysfunction.
item.description: This systematic review evaluated the clinical efficacy of lumbar roll and modified SIMS manipulation techniques for sacroiliac joint dysfunction, searching databases for studies published between January 2021 and December 2025.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: In adult patients with brainstem strokes, does dysphagia rehabilitation improve health-related outcomes compared to usual care for inpatients? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis with 12 studies and 443 participants explored the effectiveness of dysphagia rehabilitation in inpatients with brainstem stroke, categorizing interventions into traditional swallowing therapy or device inclusion.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Exploring equity in telehealth interventions in physiotherapy: A meta-epidemiological study.
item.description: A meta-epidemiological study assessed equity in physiotherapy telehealth interventions using the PROGRESS-Plus framework, including 94 telehealth equity trials, to understand factors influencing health outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Contralateral Balance Training Improves Postural Control and Functional Recovery After Arthroscopic Anterior Talofibular Ligament Repair: A Prospective, Randomized, Single-Blind Trial | Sports Medicine - Open
item.description: A prospective, randomized, single-blind trial found that contralateral balance training significantly improved postural control and functional recovery after arthroscopic anterior talofibular ligament repair.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Effects of resistance-band shoulder exercise on pain, range of motion, and functional disability in diabetic adults with adhesive capsulitis: a pilot randomized controlled trial | BMC Musculoskeletal Disorders
item.description: A pilot randomized controlled trial found that resistance-band shoulder exercise improved pain, range of motion, and functional disability in diabetic adults with adhesive capsulitis.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Effectiveness of Visual Needle Knife Release in Treating Knee Osteoarthritis: A Meta-analysis | Indian Journal of Orthopaedics
item.description: A meta-analysis evaluated the effectiveness of visual needle knife release in treating knee osteoarthritis.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Specific mode acupoint electrical stimulation combined with NGF for neurological recovery in ischemic stroke convalescence: A randomized controlled study | BMC Medicine
item.description: A randomized controlled study investigated the effect of specific mode acupoint electrical stimulation combined with nerve growth factor (NGF) for neurological recovery in ischemic stroke convalescence.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Healthcare professionals’ perspectives on implementing the H.O.M.E mobility intervention in acute care: a mixed-methods study | BMC Geriatrics
item.description: A mixed-methods study explored healthcare professionals’ experiences and perceptions of implementing the H.O.M.E mobility intervention in acute care settings for older adults.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Integrated cardiopulmonary rehabilitation nursing workforce development: an integrative literature review | BMC Nursing
item.description: An integrative literature review examined the integrated cardiopulmonary rehabilitation nursing workforce development, focusing on educational pathways, competency expectations, and implementation conditions.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Advancing spine connectomics and neural integration through machine learning and neuroengineering: a narrative review.
item.description: A narrative review summarizes recent advances in spinal circuitry, emphasizing machine learning and neuroengineering, suggesting the spinal cord is an active system contributing to sensorimotor integration and adaptive motor control.
item.author: PubMed
